reasons_to_visit.details: On the first weekend, 50,000+ runners (and many costumed walkers) take over the Old Town for Geneva's signature race — one of the largest urban races in Europe and a celebration of civic identity.

Around December 11–12, Geneva marks the failed 1602 Savoyard siege with torchlit processions through the Old Town in period dress, the breaking of chocolate cauldrons, and the recitation of 'Cé qu'è lainô' across schools and squares.
Jardin Anglais, Noël au Quai, Fusterie, and the Carouge market form a self-contained festive circuit with vin chaud, fondue chalets, and ice rinks.
Light installations across the city extend into mid-December, making after-dark walks especially atmospheric.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Ice Skating at Parc des Bastions
reasons_to_visit.details: A free outdoor ice rink runs in the historic park beneath the Old Town, with cocoa stalls and live music on weekends.
Major resorts (Chamonix, Verbier, Les Portes du Soleil) at full operation. Day trips from Geneva straightforward.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Persistent fog and overcast skies; bise wind can make outdoor time uncomfortable.
Sunset by 16:45. Plan around the dark and embrace evening illuminations.
December 24 afternoon onward, 25, and 26 see widespread shop, museum, and restaurant closures. Public transport reduced.
Escalade weekend, mid-month conferences, and New Year's Eve all drive sharp rate spikes. Mid-December weekdays are the value sweet spot.
Boats minimal; outdoor cafés closed except heated terraces.
Cold weather suspends the fountain throughout the month.

weather.precipitation: Cold and damp. Frequent fog; snow possible at city level but unusual; consistent above 800m.
weather.general_tips: Pack winter coat, gloves, hat. Grippy shoes for damp cobblestones.

tourism_level.crowds: Escalade weekend extremely busy in the Old Town; otherwise comfortable.
tourism_level.pricing: Escalade weekend spikes; otherwise mid-range with attractive mid-week deals.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Civic-festive: torchlit processions, market stalls, chocolate cauldrons, marathon energy. Geneva at its most distinctive.

events_holidays.name: Course de l'Escalade
events_holidays.description: Massive urban race weekend through the Old Town — competitive, fun-run, and costumed Marmite categories.
events_holidays.notes: Old Town traffic closures from Saturday morning. Even non-runners should visit for the atmosphere.

events_holidays.name: Fête de l'Escalade
events_holidays.description: Geneva's most loved local festival: torchlit processions in 17th-century costume through the Old Town, breaking of chocolate marmite cauldrons full of marzipan vegetables, and recitation of 'Cé qu'è lainô' (the city anthem).
events_holidays.notes: Procession routes published in advance. Wear warm clothes for outdoor torchlight evenings.

events_holidays.name: Christmas Markets Run
events_holidays.description: Jardin Anglais, Noël au Quai, Fusterie, Carouge — all open daily through December 24.

unique_experiences.activity: Course de l'Escalade Run or Spectate
unique_experiences.description: Run the 4–8 km route through cobbled streets and Reformation-era walls, or watch from Place du Bourg-de-Four with a vin chaud.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Race registration opens months ahead and fills fast.

unique_experiences.where: Old Town — Cour Saint-Pierre to Place du Bourg-de-Four
unique_experiences.description: Hundreds of locals in 17th-century costume process by torchlight, halting at squares to perform Cé qu'è lainô and serve hot vegetable soup.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Free. Arrive early at Place du Bourg-de-Four for the soup. Wear gloves.

unique_experiences.where: Chocolatiers across the city (Auer, Du Rhône, Stettler, Favarger)
unique_experiences.description: Chocolate cauldrons filled with marzipan vegetables, broken with the family recitation 'Ainsi périssent les ennemis de la République!'
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Multiple sizes; whole marmites cost meaningfully more than tasting portions.

unique_experiences.description: Free outdoor rink with rentals available; surrounded by Christmas chalets and the Reformation Wall.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Skate rental fee small; bring gloves.

weather.precipitation: Cold and damp. Snow possible at city level; common in the mountains.
weather.general_tips: Pack for cold; check holiday opening hours carefully.

tourism_level.crowds: Quieter mid-month, then climbing into Christmas week and peaking on New Year's Eve.
tourism_level.pricing: Christmas week and NYE at year-high; mid-December weekdays the value window.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Festive close to the year — markets glowing, restaurants full of long lunches, lake walks under early winter dusk.

events_holidays.name: Christmas Eve & Christmas Day
events_holidays.description: Public holiday — most shops, museums, and many restaurants closed. Lake cruises minimal; trams reduced.
events_holidays.notes: Plan hotel dining or pre-book restaurants known to open.

events_holidays.name: New Year's Eve (Saint-Sylvestre)
events_holidays.description: Lakefront fireworks (when scheduled), hotel galas, restaurant prix-fixe menus, and packed late-night bars and clubs.
events_holidays.notes: Restaurant menus often expensive and fixed. Reserve far in advance. Late-night trams operate.

unique_experiences.where: Jardin Anglais → Noël au Quai → Fusterie
unique_experiences.description: Walk between Geneva's main Christmas markets along the lakefront and into the Old Town for chalets, vin chaud, and crafts.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Cash useful; evening fairy lights best.

unique_experiences.activity: Christmas Eve Lake Walk + Hotel Dinner
unique_experiences.where: Quai du Mont-Blanc + a grand hotel restaurant
unique_experiences.description: Pre-dinner lakefront stroll under festive lighting, followed by a celebratory dinner at Le Chat-Botté, Bayview, or Il Lago.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book months ahead. Many menus are fixed.

unique_experiences.description: Reliable snow, full lift operations, and a 90-minute trip from Geneva.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Book transfers early during holiday week.

unique_experiences.where: Quai du Général-Guisan or Quai du Mont-Blanc
unique_experiences.description: Hotel galas, dinner cruises, and (some years) public fireworks over the lake.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Confirm whether public fireworks scheduled. Restaurant reservations long-since locked in.
